Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: They gave a scenario a complex order fulfillment process in a large scale distribution center They listed activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: They want a Python dictionary representing the Log Skeleton for the scenario This includes constraints equivalence always_before always_after never_together activ_freq directly_follows

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to think about typical order fulfillment process flow a typical sequence Receive order RO is the start Then check availability CA Then reserve items RI Then pick items PI Then quality check QC Then pack order PO Then generate shipping label GSL Then dispatch D Invoice IC and record payment RP could happen after dispatch

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The equivalence constraints could be something like CA RI meaning that for each case CA occurs the same number of times as RI Or we might set equivalence PO GSL because each pack order yields a shipping label Or maybe equivalence IC RP as invoice and payment record should appear same number of times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always_before constraints eg CA always before RI RI always before PI PI always before QC QC always before PO PO always before GSL GSL always before D D before IC IC before RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If we include both always_before and always_after for same pair its contradictory they cannot both hold

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: We need to choose a consistent set Lets pick always_before for sequential dependencies For always_after we can omit it if not needed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The dictionary may have empty sets for categories where there are no constraints So it is okay to have always_after set empty and never_together set empty

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Equivalence can be set of pairs CA RI and PO GSL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Activity frequencies we can define each occurs exactly once So activ_freq maps RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP to set 1

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Directly_follows can be pairs RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D and IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check consistency A pair may appear in both always_before and directly_follows and that is fine direct follow is stronger but consistent

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Final dictionary log_skeleton equivalence CA RI and PO GSL always_before RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP always_after empty set never_together empty set activ_freq all activities map to 1 directly_follows RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D IC RP
